tasks.title: Sign in to AWS Management Console

steps.text: Open the AWS Management Console in a new browser tab via Open Console.
Retain the default Account ID, enter the provided IAM Username and Password, and sign in.
Select us-east-1 (N. Virginia) from the top-right region dropdown.
Sign in to AWS Management Console

XYZ Company wants to automate a simple workflow using AWS Step Functions to process a message through a Lambda function. Your job is to create a Lambda function, define a state machine in Step Functions, test the workflow, and validate the setup. Here’s how to proceed:
Set runtime to Python 3.9. Select existing role under permission in configration
Select existing role under permission in configration
In the Lambda function code editor, write a simple function to process a message (e.g., convert a message to uppercase).
Deploy
Select Write your workflow in code and select Standard type.
Replace the existing state machine JSON code with new JSON code to invoke lambada function(Don’t forget to replace LambadaARN)
Select Standard type in config and attach a role with AwsLambadaRole and AWSLambadaBasicExecutionRole permissions
Create the state machine.
Start execution.
Provide a simple JSON input (e.g., {"message": "hello world"}).
Start the execution and wait for it to complete.
Check the execution details to verify the output
Ensure the Lambda function and Step Functions state machine are created and configured correctly.
Verify the state machine execution completed successfully with the expected output.
In the lab console, click Validate to check the setup.
Sign out of the AWS Management Console by clicking your username (top-right) and selecting Sign out.
In the lab console, click End Lab from the lab console and wait for it to complete.
You’ve completed the StepFunctions Workflow Basics challenge!
